day3_morning_wake_2.name: Me
day3_morning_wake_2.text: *...My body feels heavy. Maybe because of last night's flash—I don't feel refreshed.*

day3_morning_wake_3.name: Me
day3_morning_wake_3.text: *I stood in front of the sink to wash my face. The mirror is fogged up. I reached out to wipe it... and stopped.*

day3_morning_mirror_1.name: Me
day3_morning_mirror_1.text: *...For some reason, I don't want to look in the mirror. I don't know why.*

day3_morning_mirror_2.name: Me
day3_morning_mirror_2.text: *I just splashed water on my face and left.*

day3_morning_mirror_3.name: Me
day3_morning_mirror_3.text: *I opened the fridge and... the milk I bought yesterday has been moved.*

day3_morning_milk_2.name: Me
day3_morning_milk_2.text: *...The pencil case, the milk. Things are moving without me knowing.*

day3_morning_commute_1.name: Me
day3_morning_commute_1.text: *I leave the house and head to school. The morning air is cold.*

day3_morning_commute_2.name: Me
day3_morning_commute_2.text: *Walking through the alley, two students are walking ahead of me. One of them says:*

day3_morning_commute_3.name: Girl student A
day3_morning_commute_3.text: "Hey, did you do the math homework? Number 2 was seriously hard."

day3_morning_commute_4.name: Girl student B
day3_morning_commute_4.text: "Lol, I just copied the answer key~"

day3_morning_commute_6.name: Me
day3_morning_commute_6.text: *This conversation. I heard it yesterday. Same alley, same time, same two people. **Same lines**.*

day3_morning_commute_7.name: Me
day3_morning_commute_7.text: *...No. They probably have similar conversations every day. It's normal for students to talk about homework.*

day3_morning_commute_8.name: Me
day3_morning_commute_8.text: *But my pace quickens. I don't know why.*

day3_morning_gate_1.name: Me
day3_morning_gate_1.text: *I arrived at school. Today, Sea isn't waiting for me.*

day3_morning_gate_2.name: Me
day3_morning_gate_2.text: *...That actually bothers me more. She was waiting yesterday.*

day3_morning_gate_3.name: Me
day3_morning_gate_3.text: *I walk through the school gate among other students. I thought someone called me from behind and turned around.*

day3_morning_gate_4.name: Me
day3_morning_gate_4.text: *Nobody was looking. Everyone was walking forward.*

day3_morning_gate_5.name: Me
day3_morning_gate_5.text: *...But that boy who just passed by. The way he looked at me... it wasn't how you look at a stranger. It was more like 'Oh, you're here again.'*

day3_morning_locker_2.name: Me
day3_morning_locker_2.text: *A locker. The name has been scratched off. Marks of being scraped repeatedly with a key or knife.*

day3_morning_locker_3.name: Me
day3_morning_locker_3.text: *Curious, I opened the locker. It wasn't locked. Completely empty. Just dust.*

day3_morning_locker_4.name: Me
day3_morning_locker_4.text: *In the corner, a torn photo. A passport-sized photo cut in half. Only half a face remains.*

day3_morning_locker_5.name: Me
day3_morning_locker_5.text: *...This face. I feel like I've seen it before. Who is it?*

day3_morning_photo_1.name: Me
day3_morning_photo_1.text: *I put the photo in my pocket.*

day3_morning_photo_2.name: Me
day3_morning_photo_2.text: *...And there's one more scratched inscription on the inside wall of the locker. Very small.*

day3_morning_inscription_1.name: Me
day3_morning_inscription_1.text: *The handwriting... it's my handwriting. The same as what I saw under the desk yesterday.*

day3_morning_inscription_2.name: Me
day3_morning_inscription_2.text: *...Lots of people have similar handwriting. It's probably just a coincidence.*

day3_morning_inscription_3.name: Me
day3_morning_inscription_3.text: *I closed the locker and turned the corner. Seolhwa is standing there. Close this time.*

day3_morning_seolhwa_2.name: Me
day3_morning_seolhwa_2.text: *My heart races. Seolhwa opens her mouth.*

day3_morning_seolhwa_4.name: Me
day3_morning_seolhwa_4.text: "...Huh? Yeah, I am. How did you know?"

day3_morning_seolhwa_5.name: Me
day3_morning_seolhwa_5.text: *Seolhwa doesn't answer. A complicated expression. Then, in a small voice:*

day3_morning_seolhwa_8.name: Me
day3_morning_seolhwa_8.text: *Seolhwa turns and walks away. She doesn't answer.*

day3_morning_seolhwa_9.name: Me
day3_morning_seolhwa_9.text: *...Be careful of what? And how did she know I'm left-handed? I never mentioned it when we greeted each other yesterday.*

day3_morning_seolhwa_10.name: Me
day3_morning_seolhwa_10.text: *I turned to look again. She had already disappeared around the end of the hallway.*

day3_morning_classroom_1.name: Me
day3_morning_classroom_1.text: *I walked down the hallway and stood in front of the classroom. I can hear murmuring beyond the door.*

day3_morning_classroom_3.name: Me
day3_morning_classroom_3.text: *The conversations stopped. All at once. Like a knife cutting through them.*

day3_morning_classroom_4.name: Me
day3_morning_classroom_4.text: *This happened yesterday too. But today... it's more obvious. Yesterday I wondered if I was being oversensitive, but today I'm sure.*

day3_morning_classroom_5.name: Me
day3_morning_classroom_5.text: *Two boys in the front row glance at each other. A girl in the back row turns to look out the window. Nobody is looking at me, but everyone is aware of me.*

day3_morning_nametag_1.name: Me
day3_morning_nametag_1.text: *...My gaze drops to the name tag on my chest. My name. Three syllables I've pinned on every morning.*

day3_morning_nametag_2.name: Me
day3_morning_nametag_2.text: *— Suddenly it feels foreign, like a word from another language. I touched it with my fingers. Only the cold feel of plastic came back.*

day3_morning_sea_1.name: Me
day3_morning_sea_1.text: *...I get goosebumps. This isn't me being oversensitive. Something is wrong.*

day3_morning_sea_4.name: Han Sea
day3_morning_sea_4.text: "You don't look well. Couldn't sleep last night?"

day3_morning_sea_5.name: Me
day3_morning_sea_5.text: *I look at Sea. The corners of her mouth are turned up. Her voice is bright. But her eyes are... different.*

day3_morning_sea_6.name: Me
day3_morning_sea_6.text: *Those aren't eyes of 'worry'—they're eyes of 'checking.' She's not worried about whether I'm okay. She's **inspecting** my condition.*

day3_morning_sea_7.name: Me
day3_morning_sea_7.text: "Yeah, I tossed and turned a bit."

day3_morning_sea_9.name: Me
day3_morning_sea_9.text: *Sea turns back in her seat. The muscles in the back of her neck are tense. ...At least, that's how it feels to me. I'm probably just being oversensitive again.*

day3_morning_pencil_1.name: Me
day3_morning_pencil_1.text: *I sit down and open the desk drawer... the pencil case I left yesterday has been moved.*

day3_morning_pencil_2.name: Me
day3_morning_pencil_2.text: *I definitely put it on the left, but it's on the right.*

day3_morning_end.name: Me
day3_morning_end.text: *Class begins. I try to focus, but my mind is in turmoil.*
